title: Biometrics Where to give your fingerprints and photo - Canada.ca
content:
## Where to give your fingerprints and photo
You need to make an appointment to give your biometrics.
- Appointments are free to book .
- Book your appointment as soon as you receive your biometric instruction letter (BIL) to avoid delays. You have 30 days from the time you get your BIL to give your biometrics. Read the Important information section of your BIL for more information.
- You have 30 days from the time you get your BIL to give your biometrics.
- Read the Important information section of your BIL for more information.
If you can’t given your biometrics before your BIL expires, use the web form to tell us why you need more time.
Make sure you provide details about your situation.
For example, if you weren’t able to get an appointment by the deadline in your BIL,
- tell us the date of your appointment
- upload a screenshot of the appointment confirmation

## Giving your biometrics outside Canada (except the US)
You can give your biometrics at a visa application centre (VAC) by making an appointment.
- Biometrics appointments are free .
- Don’t pay anyone for an appointment.
Before you go, make sure to visit the VAC’s website to find out what services are available. All VACs have
      biometric collection services.
If there's no VAC in the country or territory you're applying from, you can go to any other VAC
         location.

## Giving your biometrics in the US
You can give your biometrics at an application support centre (ASC) or a visa application centre (VAC).
- You must already be legally in the US to go to an ASC or a VAC.
- Biometrics appointments are free
- Don’t pay anyone to book an appointment

### How to book an appointment at an ASC
To book an appointment at an ASC, use the US Citizenship and Immigration Services website.

### How to book an appointment at a VAC
To book an appointment to give your biometrics at a VAC , use the VAC website for the location
      where you plan to go.
Before you visit a VAC, make sure to check the VAC’s website to find out what services are available. All VACs
      have biometric collection services.
Collection site location
Los Angeles, United States VAC
United States
                  VACs
New York, United States VAC
United States
                  VACs

## Giving your biometrics in Canada
You can give your biometrics at a designated Service Canada office .
      You can find a location and make your appointment to give biometrics using the eServiceCanada Appointment
      Booking Tool.
- Don’t go to a Service Canada office unless you have a scheduled appointment.
Your fingerprints must be taken electronically through
- your local police service in Canada
- the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP), or
- an RCMP-accredited fingerprinting company
Do not go to a Service Canada Centre
We don’t accept fingerprints taken at a Service Canada Centre for citizenship applications. If you get them taken at a Service Canada Centre
- we can’t use them for your citizenship application and they’ll be deleted
- you’ll need to have your fingerprints retaken at one of the above collection points

## Giving your biometrics at a port of entry
You can only give your biometrics at the port of entry when you arrive in Canada if you meet both of the following requirements:
- You’re eligible to apply for a study
         permit or work
         permit at a port of entry.
- You start and finish your application at the port of entry. You can’t give your biometrics when you enter Canada if you already submitted an
               application and got a BIL. In this case, you must use one of the other biometrics service providers.
- You can’t give your biometrics when you enter Canada if you already submitted an
               application and got a BIL. In this case, you must use one of the other biometrics service providers.
- In this case, you must use one of the other biometrics service providers.
Make sure you’re eligible before you go.
